title: भगवद्गीता अध्याय १० - विभूतियोग
devanagariText:
॥ अथ दशमोऽध्यायः - विभूतियोगः ॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
भूय एव महाबाहो शृणु मे परमं वचः।
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया॥१॥

न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः॥२॥

यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम्।
असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥३॥

बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः।
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च॥४॥

अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः।
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः॥५॥

महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा।
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः॥६॥

एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।
सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः॥७॥

अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते।
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः॥८॥

मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम्।
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च॥९॥

तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम्।
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते॥१०॥

तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः।
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता॥११॥

अर्जुन उवाच —
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान्।
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्॥१२॥

आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा।
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे॥१३॥

सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव।
न हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः॥१४॥

स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम।
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते॥१५॥

वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि॥१६॥

कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्।
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया॥१७॥

विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन।
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि शृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्॥१८॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।
प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे॥१९॥

अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च॥२०॥

आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्।
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी॥२१॥

वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः।
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना॥२२॥

रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्।
वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम्॥२३॥

पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्।
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः॥२४॥

महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम्।
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः॥२५॥

अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः।
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः॥२६॥

सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः॥२८॥

प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम्।
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम्॥३०॥

पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम्।
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी॥३१॥

सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन।
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम्॥३२॥

अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च।
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः॥३३॥

मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम्।
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा॥३४॥

यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा।
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम्॥४१॥

अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन।
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत्॥४२॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भगवद्गीतासूपनिषत्सु ब्रह्मविद्यायां योगशास्त्रे
श्रीकृष्णार्जुनसंवादे विभूतियोगो नाम दशमोऽध्यायः ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Dashamo'dhyayah - Vibhuti Yogah ||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Bhuya Eva Maha-Baho Shrinu Me Paramam Vachah.
Yat-Te'ham Priyamanaya Vakshyami Hita-Kamyaya.||1||

Na Me Viduh Sura-Ganah Prabhavam Na Maharshayah.
Aham-Adir-Hi Devanam Maharshinam Cha Sarvashah.||2||

Yo Mam-Ajam-Anadim Cha Vetti Loka-Maheshvaram.
Asammuddhah Sa Martyeshu Sarva-Papaih Pramuchyate.||3||

Aham Sarvasya Prabhavo Mattah Sarvam Pravartate.
Iti Matva Bhajante Mam Budha Bhava-Samanvitah.||8||

Tesham Satata-Yuktanam Bhajatam Priti-Purvakam.
Dadami Buddhi-Yogam Tam Yena Mam-Upayanti Te.||10||

Tesham-Eva-Anukampa-Artham-Aham-Ajnana-Jam Tamah.
Nashayamy-Atma-Bhava-Stho Jnana-Dipena Bhasvata.||11||

Arjuna Uvacha —
Param Brahma Param Dhama Pavitram Paramam Bhavan.
Purusham Shashvatam Divyam-Adi-Devam-Ajam Vibhum.||12||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Aham-Atma Gudakesha Sarva-Bhuta-Ashaya-Sthitah.
Aham-Adish-Cha Madhyam Cha Bhutanam-Anta Eva Cha.||20||

Adityanam-Aham Vishnur-Jyotisham Ravir-Amshuman.
Marichir-Marutam-Asmi Nakshatranam-Aham Shashi.||21||

Vedanam Sama-Vedo'smi Devanam-Asmi Vasavah.
Indriyanam Manash-Cha-Asmi Bhutanam-Asmi Chetana.||22||

Maharishinam Bhrigur-Aham Giram-Asmy-Ekam-Aksharam.
Yajnanam Japa-Yajno'smi Sthavaranam Himalayah.||25||

Prahladash-Cha-Asmi Daityanam Kalah Kalayatam-Aham.
Mriganam Cha Mrigendro'ham Vainateyash-Cha Pakshinam.||30||

Pavanah Pavatam-Asmi Ramah Shastra-Bhritam-Aham.
Jhashanam Makarash-Cha-Asmi Srotasam-Asmi Jahnavi.||31||

Akshranam-Akaro'smi Dvandvah Samasikasya Cha.
Aham-Eva-Akshayah Kalo Dhata-Aham Vishvato-Mukhah.||33||

Yad-Yad Vibhutimat-Sattvam Shrimad-Urjitam-Eva Va.
Tat-Tad-Eva-Avagachchha Tvam Mama Tejo'msha-Sambhavam.||41||

Athava Bahuna-Itena Kim Jnatena Tava-Arjuna.
Vishtabhya-Aham-Idam Kritsnam-Eka-Amshena Sthito Jagat.||42||

|| Iti Shrimad-Bhagavad-Gitasu-Upanishatsu Brahma-Vidyayam Yoga-Shastre
Shri-Krishna-Arjuna-Samvade Vibhuti-Yogo Nama Dashamo'dhyayah ||
hindiMeaning: दशम अध्याय 'विभूतियोग' में श्रीकृष्ण अपनी दिव्य विभूतियों (ऐश्वर्यों) का वर्णन करते हैं। वे कहते हैं — मेरे प्रभाव को न देवता जानते हैं, न महर्षि, क्योंकि मैं सबका आदि हूँ। मैं सबकी उत्पत्ति का कारण हूँ, मुझसे सब प्रवृत्त होता है। जो प्रेमपूर्वक सतत मेरा भजन करते हैं, उन्हें मैं बुद्धियोग देता हूँ। अर्जुन के अनुरोध पर कृष्ण अपनी प्रमुख विभूतियाँ बताते हैं — आदित्यों में विष्णु, ज्योतियों में सूर्य, वेदों में सामवेद, इन्द्रियों में मन, महर्षियों में भृगु, वाणी में एकाक्षर ॐ, यज्ञों में जपयज्ञ, पर्वतों में हिमालय, वृक्षों में पीपल, शस्त्रधारियों में राम, नदियों में गंगा, अक्षरों में 'अ'। अंत में कहते हैं — जो भी विभूतिमान, श्रीमान, बलवान वस्तु है, उसे मेरे तेज का अंश जानो। मैं अपने एक अंश मात्र से सम्पूर्ण जगत को धारण किए हुए हूँ।
benefits: Reveals the divine glories and manifestations of God in creation
Develops the ability to see God in all excellent things
Contains the beautiful teaching: Aham Sarvasya Prabhavo
Strengthens devotion through recognizing God's presence everywhere
Invokes the vision to see the sacred in the ordinary
Inspires awe and wonder at the majesty of the Divine
description: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10 - Vibhuti Yoga — भगवद्गीता अध्याय १० - विभूतियोग — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Krishna. Drawn from Bhagavad Gita - Mahabharata, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to strengthen devotion through recognizing God's presence everywhere and inspire awe and wonder at the majesty of the Divine. It is also said to develop the ability to see God in all excellent things. This sacred text is ideally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami for enhanced blessings. Comprising 42 verses, it can be completed in approximately 18 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10 - Vibhuti Yoga in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10 — Vibhuti Yoga?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10 (भगवद्गीता अध्याय १०), titled Vibhuti Yoga (the Yoga of Divine Glories), is the tenth chapter of the 700-verse Srimad Bhagavad Gita, containing 42 verses. In this chapter Lord Krishna reveals to Arjuna his vibhutis — his principal manifestations among the categories of created beings — declaring that whatever is glorious, beautiful, powerful or excellent in any class of beings is to be understood as a spark of his own splendour. He lists himself as the Atman seated in the heart of all beings, Vishnu among the Adityas, the radiant sun among lights, Marichi among the Maruts, the moon among the stars, Sama among the Vedas, Indra among the gods, the mind among the senses, consciousness among living beings, Shiva among the Rudras, Kubera among the Yakshas, Agni among the Vasus, Meru among mountains, Brihaspati among priests, and dozens more. (Source: Srimad Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10; Mahabharata Bhishma Parva.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Vibhuti and why is this chapter called Vibhuti Yoga?
seoFaqs.answer: Vibhuti (विभूति) means divine power, glory, splendour, opulence, or majestic manifestation. In Vedanta, vibhutis are the special manifestations through which the otherwise formless and infinite Brahman becomes accessible to the meditator's mind. The chapter is called Vibhuti Yoga because Krishna teaches that the meditator who cannot grasp the unmanifest Brahman directly should meditate on whatever is most glorious in any category of being and realise it as Krishna himself — vibhuter-vistara is yoga, the path of meditation through divine glories. Verse 10.41 declares: yad-yad vibhutimat-sattvam srimad-urjitameva va, tat-tad-evavagachcha tvam mama tejomsha-sambhavam — Whatever being is glorious, prosperous, or powerful, know that to have sprung from but a fraction of my splendour. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 10.41; Shankara Bhashya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous verse Aham Atma Gudakesha in Chapter 10?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 10.20 — Aham atma Gudakesha sarva-bhutashaya-sthitah, aham adish-cha madhyam cha bhutanam anta eva cha — is the central declaration of Vibhuti Yoga: I am the Atman, O Gudakesha (conqueror of sleep — Arjuna), seated in the hearts of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and also the end of all beings. This is the foundational verse from which all the subsequent vibhutis flow — Krishna first establishes himself as the Self of all, and then identifies himself with the supreme expression in every category. The verse is one of the most quoted in Vedanta literature and forms the basis of antaryamin meditation (the Lord as the inner Self). It is the principal verse for daily Krishna-meditation in many Vaishnava sampradayas. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 10.20; Sri Vaishnava Bhagavad Vishaya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are some of the principal vibhutis (divine glories) listed in Chapter 10?
seoFaqs.answer: Krishna lists his principal vibhutis in verses 10.21-10.39: among the Adityas I am Vishnu, among lights the radiant sun, among the Maruts I am Marichi, among the stars I am the moon; among the Vedas I am Sama Veda, among gods I am Vasava (Indra), among the senses I am the mind, in beings I am consciousness; among the Rudras I am Shankara, among Yakshas Kubera, among Vasus Pavaka (fire), among mountains Meru; among priests Brihaspati, among generals Skanda, among water-bodies the ocean; among the great rishis Bhrigu, among words Om-Ekaksharam, among yajnas the japa-yajna, among immovables the Himalayas; among trees the Ashvattha (peepal), among devarshis Narada, among gandharvas Chitraratha, among siddhas Kapila Muni; among horses Uchchaihsravas, among elephants Airavata, among men the king; among weapons the Vajra, among cows Kamadhenu; among progenitors I am Kandarpa (Kama, the divine principle of creation); among serpents Vasuki, among nagas Ananta, among demigod judges Yama; among Daityas Prahlada, among reckoners time; among beasts the lion, among birds Garuda; among purifiers the wind, among warriors Rama, among fish the Makara, among rivers the Ganga; among sciences the science of the Self, among debaters the conclusive logic; among letters the letter A (akshara akaro'smi), among compounds the dvandva; among months Margashirsha, among seasons Vasanta. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 10.21-10.39.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10 traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 10 is recited on Day 10 of an 18-day Gita Parayana cycle. It is especially recited for prosperity, excellence, leadership and success in any chosen field — devotees meditate on the relevant vibhuti (a student on Sama Veda or japa-yajna, a soldier on Skanda or the Vajra, a businessman on Kubera or Kamadhenu, a king on the king vibhuti, a poet on Narada or Brihaspati). The chapter is also chanted on Gita Jayanti (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi — Krishna himself declares Margashirsha to be supreme month: masanam Margashirsho'ham, 10.35), Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna's day), and Ekadashi. The Sri Vaishnava tradition gives it special place because of the central antaryamin verse (10.20). (Sources: Gita Mahatmya; classical Parayana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Krishna say I am the letter A among letters in Chapter 10?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 10.33 contains the famous declaration: aksharanam akaro'smi — among letters I am the letter A. The akara (अ) is the first sound of the Sanskrit alphabet, the first sound the human throat naturally produces (the throat-vowel), the first letter of Om (which is a-u-m), and the seed-sound from which all other speech arises. By identifying himself with the akara, Krishna declares himself the source and beginning of all sound, all language, all Veda — paralleling the Mandukya Upanishad's teaching on Om. This verse is therefore the pratah-smarana (morning meditation) of mantra-japakas and Vedic scholars. The same verse continues: dvandvah samasikasya cha — among compound words I am the dvandva (the equal-paired compound) — symbolising harmony of dualities. (Sources: Bhagavad Gita 10.33; Mandukya Upanishad; Shankara Bhashya.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Chapter 10 connect to the verse-by-verse Gita pages on this site?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 10 — Vibhuti Yoga contains 42 verses, each of which is studied verse-by-verse on this site at /gita/chapter-10/verse-1 through /gita/chapter-10/verse-42. The verse-level pages provide the Devanagari, transliteration, word-by-word meaning, English translation, Hindi anvaya and commentary by classical acharyas for each shloka — particularly important for Chapter 10 because each vibhuti verse contains a separate cosmology and theology. This stotra page presents the entire chapter as a single text for parayana, while the verse pages support meditation on individual vibhutis. Devotees often select 1-3 vibhutis most relevant to their svadharma (own duty) for daily upasana. The chapter is the perfect bridge to Chapter 11 (Vishvarupa Darshana Yoga) where these vibhutis become a single cosmic vision. (Source: Stotra and Gita parayana traditions.)
